examples.title: Basic COCOMO Effort Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate effort using Basic COCOMO model
examples.code:
FUNCTION CalculateBasicCOCOMO(kloc, mode)
 // mode: 1=Organic, 2=Semi-detached, 3=Embedded
 
 IF mode = 1 THEN // Organic
 a = 2.4
 b = 1.05
 ELSE IF mode = 2 THEN // Semi-detached
 a = 3.0
 b = 1.12
 ELSE // Embedded
 a = 3.6
 b = 1.20
 END IF
 
 // Calculate effort in person-months
 effort = a * (kloc ^ b)
 
 RETURN effort
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Basic COCOMO uses simple formula: Effort = a × (KLOC)^b, where a and b vary by project mode. Result is in person-months (PM).

examples.title: Function Point Analysis Algorithm
examples.description: Step-by-step FP counting and adjustment calculation
examples.explanation: FP analysis counts user-functional requirements, assigns complexity weights, then adjusts using 14 General System Characteristics to get final FP count.

examples.title: Intermediate COCOMO with Cost Drivers
examples.explanation: Intermediate COCOMO multiplies base effort by product of all cost driver multipliers, providing more accurate estimates than Basic model.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n=number of cost drivers

examples.title: Use Case Points Estimation
examples.description: UCP calculation with technical and environmental factors
examples.explanation: UCP estimates effort based on use case analysis - counts actors and use cases with complexity, then applies TCF and ECF to get final UCP for effort calculation.
